Notice Of Motion And Motion Appointing A Partition Referee Per Ccp 873.010
Off Calendar Jul-26-2019 Continued To Aug-09-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LAWRENCE DORFMAN'S Motion Appointing A Partition Referee Per Ccp 873.010.

The joint report filed by counsel on July 23, 2019 is not what the court ordered. The July 23 report cursorily describes a failed meet-and-confer. The court ordered a report "stating each side's views of individual referee candidates." Thus, the parties are ordered to file, on or before August 2, a new joint report that sets out in three separate headings the name of each candidate. Immediately following the heading, the party advocating the candidate is to describe why he or she should be appointed. Following that, the party opposing the appointment is to describe why the candidate should not be appointed. The same format is to be used for each of the three candidates. Resumes may be attached to the joint report. The hearing on appointing a partition referee is continued to August 9, 2019.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Off Calendar May-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
A. Jeanne Grove, Esq., Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck, LLP Telephone 415-926-7630, appearing for Plaintiff;
Richard Wallace, Esq., Briscoe Ivester & Bazel, LLP, Telephone 415-402-2700, appearing for Defendant Richard Carter;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Dorfman's motion for summary adjudication and request for an interlocutory judgment of partition is granted.

"'A co-owner of property has an absolute right to partition unless barred by a valid waiver.'" (Orien v. Lutz (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 957, 962; Code Civ. Proc. 872.710(b).) "If the court finds that the plaintiff is entitled to partition, it shall make an interlocutory judgment that determines the interests of the parties in the property and orders the partition of the property." (Code Civ. Proc. 872.720(a).) (** See word mintues for complete details **)

Order is signed.
